headline: Ombudsman proposal shelved for the 6th time since 2008
context: A parliamentary ombudsman bill was shelved for the sixth time in January 2026, with the government citing 'insufficient consensus' despite cross-party support.

cards.big: Parliamentary ombudsman proposed 6 times since 2008 — shelved every single time
cards.sub: 18 years of trying to create a citizens' complaint mechanism against government

cards.big: Malaysia has no independent ombudsman at federal or state level
cards.sub: Citizens have no dedicated avenue for complaints about government maladministration

cards.big: Public Complaints Bureau received 52,000 complaints in 2025 but resolved only 34%
cards.sub: Existing complaint mechanism is overwhelmed and has no enforcement power

cards.big: 120 countries worldwide have established national ombudsman institutions
cards.sub: Malaysia is in the shrinking minority of democracies without one

cards.big: Shelving an ombudsman 6 times isn't indecision — it's a decision to avoid accountability
Why does every Malaysian government promise an ombudsman but never deliver one?
